summary:
The safe early detection involves using A at about 6 weeks via TVS confirming heart activity; other tests are less reliable or invasive earlier than this timeframe.
 Agent2's reasoning (Final): Early diagnosis of pregnancy can be made most accurately through \boxed{T} between 4-5 weeks post-conception when an embryo sac without yolk sac but visible cardiac activity indicates viability. This surpasses blood/hormone testing which may not definitively confirm implantation until after that time frame due to lower sensitivity compared to imaging techniques.
 Therefore, T provides both accuracy & safety as it confirms active development before hormone-based assessments become conclusive enough.

Summary: For accurate and safe early confirmation, Trans-vaginal sonography detects embryonic structures including cardiac activity from approximately 4-5 wks post conception better than biochemical markers alone during very early stages of pregnancy.
 To summarize only the agent’s reasoning regarding the earliest safe diagnostic approach for pregnancy into two concise sentences focusing on critical clinical aspects:

The earliest safe determination of pregnancy comes from transvaginal ultrasonography detecting cardiac activity around 4-5 weeks post-conception, offering more definitive evidence sooner than hormonal assays while ensuring minimal invasiveness. Blood or urine tests cannot conclusively diagnose live pregnancies so soon because they lack sufficient specificity within such an initial window.
